cleavage agent: digested with trypsin (Sigma-Aldrich, St. Louis, MO, USA)
collision energy: a maximum injection time of 240 ms, and 28 eV normalized collision energy
fractionation method: a poly (styrene divinylbenzene) reversed-phase (RP) StageTip purification protocol was used
fragmentation method: HCD fragmentations were performed for the 10 most abundant ion signals
instrument: the final proteomic analysis was performed with a Q-Exactive Plus (Thermo Fisher Scientific) mass spectrometer
ionization type: generated on a Bruker ultrafleXtreme TOF/TOF mass spectrometer (Bruker Daltonik GmbH, Bremen, Germany) in reflectron positive mode
